childMarkdownRemark.rawMarkdownBody:
Hello! 👋 my name is Bailey and I am a **senior** studying **computer science** at the **University of Illinois**. My main interests are in cloud computing and serverless backends, applied machine learning, and quantum computing! I just finished a Winter software engineering internship at the quantitative hedge fund [Citadel](https://www.citadel.com), and am currently at [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com) for the Summer. I will graduate in May 2021 and am actively looking for new graduate software engineering and machine learning roles!

During my free time, I am an amateur road cyclist 🚲 and love cooking 🥘 with my friends and family.

## Background
I originally started college as a physics major and fell in love with the unintuitiveness of quantum physics. Except, I still wanted to work on projects that could have clear, meaningful impact on day-to-day lives--which would not have been very tangible as a quantum physicist. So, even though I made the switch to computer science, I still kept true to my roots and took up interest in quantum computing. My school did not have any resources available for the field at the time, so I created [QUIUC](https://www.quiuc.org) as an undergraduate quantum computing focus group so that I could learn together with my peers.

## Moving Away from Physics
Though I am excited for quantum computing's potential, I also recognize that there is a long road ahead before the promised exponential speed-ups in medical research and AI will be within grasp 🚀. As I had mentioned before, I am an engineer at the end of the day. So until quantum computing can be fully realized, I have found my place in application backend development.

## Cloud Computing and the Overheard App
Over the past couple years, I have taken increased interest in cloud computing and databases. Initially I needed to use AWS as a means to host small projects or get things done. Over time though, the projects became more complex and I began to go deeper into understanding what I was using. In particular, the serverless stack with AWS is where I have focused due to its flexibility, scalability, and ease-of-use. It was from this that I decided to develop the [Overheard App](https://www.overheardapp.com) with my roommate [Jeff Taylor-Chang](https://www.jefftc.com) as a deep dive into the field. Through this I have become proficient in the [Serverless Framework](https://www.serverless.com), [MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com), [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/), and the [oAuth 2.0 authentication protocol](https://oauth.net/2/). I have also attained my first [Amazon AWS Cloud Computing certification](https://aws.amazon.com/certification/) and am studying for the next exam.
